zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Booth於1473年6月11日出生在英國爾鄧翰 梅西時，他的父親George Booth Lord Of Dunham28歲，母親Catherine de Monfort32歲。他於1486年5月與Margaret Ashton在英國英格蘭藍卡爾結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1519年11月9日在英國爾鄧翰 奧 馮 希爾享年46歲，埋葬在英國爾維爾姆斯洛福。

nameMeanings.name: Booth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern): topographic or occupational name from Middle English bothe (Old Danish bōth) ‘temporary shelter, such as a covered market stall or a cattle-herdsman's hut’. The latter sense was predominant in the Pennines of Lancashire and Yorkshire, where there were many cattle farms or vaccaries, and whose subdivisions were known as ‘booths’. The principal meaning of the surname there was therefore probably ‘cattle herdsman’, ‘man in charge of a vaccary’, and thus identical with Boothman . Elsewhere it may have denoted a shopkeeper who owned a temporary market stall, but no evidence has been found to confirm this use of the surname. In the British Isles the surname is still more common in northern England, where Scandinavian influence was more marked, and in Scotland, where the word was borrowed into Gaelic as both(an).
History: Robert Booth (1604–72) is mentioned in the colonial records of Exeter, NH, in 1645. He subsequently moved to ME.

variants.name: Boothman
meanings: English (northern): occupational name from Middle English both (Old Danish bōth) ‘temporary shelter, such as a covered market stall or a cattle-herdsman's hut’ + man. In the Pennine uplands of northern England, where booths were subdivisons of vaccaries or cattle-rearing establishment, the name probably denoted a herdsman who looked after cattle, perhaps a man in charge of a vaccary. Elsewhere the surname might have denoted a shopkeeper but evidence for this lacking. Alternatively, the name can also be an occupational name for a servant of a man surnamed Booth .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Booth was born on 11 June 1473, in Dunham Massey, Cheshire, England, his father, George Booth Lord Of Dunham, was 28 and his mother, Catherine de Monfort, was 32. He married Margaret Ashton in May 1486, in Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 9 November 1519, in Dunham on the Hill, Cheshire, England, at the age of 46, and was buried in Wilmslow, Cheshire, England.

Il a épousé Margaret Ashton en mai 1486, à Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. ko.Dunham, Massey, Cheshire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 던함  테 힐
ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Booth(이)가 1473년 6월 11일 영국 체실르 던함 마시에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 George Booth Lord Of Dunham(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Catherine de Monfort(은)는 32세였다. 그는 1486년 5월 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르에서 Margaret Ashton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1519년 11월 9일 영국 체실르 던함  테 힐에서 46세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 체실르 윌슬로에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Booth родился 11 июня 1473 г. в местности дунам масси, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени George Booth Lord Of Dunham было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Catherine de Monfort, 32. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Ashton май 1486 г. в местности ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер 9 ноября 1519 г. в местности дунам он т хилл, чешир, Англия в возрасте 46 лет и был похоронен в местности уилмслоу, чешир, Англия.
